devanagari: देवहूतिरुवाच । लक्षणं महदादीनां प्रकृतेः पुरुषस्य च । स्वरूपं लक्ष्यतेऽमीषां येन तत्पारमार्थिकम् ॥ १ ॥
english_devnagari: devahūtiruvācalakṣaṇaṃ mahadādīnāṃ prakṛteḥ puruṣasya casvarūpaṃ lakṣyate'mīṣāṃ yena tatpāramārthikam
translation:
Devahūti said:
Oh Lord, the accurate description of the Mahat and other principles and of the Prakṛti and Puruṣa as described in the Sāṃkhya-Śāstra has been narrated—the accurate description by which the real nature of these (Sāṃkhya) principles is correctly understood. It is said to be the source of the Bhakti Yoga. Please tell (explain) to me in details the path of the Bhakti-yoga.

devanagari: यथा साङ्ख्येषु कथितं यन्मूलं तत्प्रचक्षते । भक्तियोगस्य मे मार्गं ब्रूहि विस्तरशः प्रभो ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: yathā sāṅkhyeṣu kathitaṃ yanmūlaṃ tatpracakṣatebhaktiyogasya me mārgaṃ brūhi vistaraśaḥ prabho

devanagari: विरागो येन पुरुषो भगवन् सर्वतो भवेत् । आचक्ष्व जीवलोकस्य विविधा मम संसृतीः ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: virāgo yena puruṣo bhagavan sarvato bhavetācakṣva jīvalokasya vividhā mama saṃsṛtīḥ
translation: Oh revered Kapila, please tell me the different types of births (existences) of this world of living beings, by hearing which a man becomes free from attachment to everything (and everywhere) in the world.

devanagari: कालस्येश्वररूपस्य परेषां च परस्य ते । स्वरूपं बत कुर्वन्ति यद्धेतोः कुशलं जनाः ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: kālasyeśvararūpasya pareṣāṃ ca parasya tesvarūpaṃ bata kurvanti yaddhetoḥ kuśalaṃ janāḥ
translation: Please tell me about the nature of your all-powerful Kāla or Time. He is regarded as the controller of the great (gods like Brahmā). It is because (of the fear of) Time that people take to good actions.

devanagari: लोकस्य मिथ्याभिमतेरचक्षुषः । चिरं प्रसुप्तस्य तमस्यनाश्रये । श्रान्तस्य कर्मस्वनुविद्धया धिया । त्वमाविरासीः किल योगभास्करः ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: lokasya mithyābhimateracakṣuṣaḥciraṃ prasuptasya tamasyanāśrayeśrāntasya karmasvanuviddhayā dhiyātvamāvirāsīḥ kila yogabhāskaraḥ
translation: You have certainly manifested yourself as the Sun shedding light on the Yoga, in order to awaken the people who are ignorant and hence full of ahaṃkāra (pride) about unreal objects (like their body etc.) and are exhausted due to the attachment of their minds to actions (karmas) and are hence fast asleep for a long time in the unending darkness of saṃsāra.

devanagari: मैत्रेय उवाच । इति मातुर्वचः श्लक्ष्णं प्रतिनन्द्य महामुनिः । आबभाषे कुरुश्रेष्ठ प्रीतस्तां करुणार्दितः ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: maitreya uvācaiti māturvacaḥ ślakṣṇaṃ pratinandya mahāmuniḥābabhāṣe kuruśreṣṭha prītastāṃ karuṇārditaḥ
translation:
Maitreya said:
Oh prominent Kuru! The great sage hailed with joy the beautiful speech of his mother. Being pleased with her and out of compassion (for her), he spoke to her.

devanagari: श्रीभगवानुवाच । भक्तियोगो बहुविधो मार्गैर्भामिनि भाव्यते । स्वभावगुणमार्गेण पुंसां भावो विभिद्यते ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: śrībhagavānuvācabhaktiyogo bahuvidho mārgairbhāmini bhāvyatesvabhāvaguṇamārgeṇa puṃsāṃ bhāvo vibhidyate
translation:
The Lord said:
Oh mother, the path of Bhakti is regarded as having many branches; for the objects of men differ according to their natural dispositions and attributes (like sattva etc.)

devanagari: अभिसन्धाय यो हिंसां दम्भं मात्सर्यमेव वा । संरम्भी भिन्नदृग्भावं मयि कुर्यात्स तामसः ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: abhisandhāya yo hiṃsāṃ dambhaṃ mātsaryameva vāsaṃrambhī bhinnadṛgbhāvaṃ mayi kuryātsa tāmasaḥ
translation: He who becomes my devotee with the intention of doing injury (to others) or out of hypocrisy and jealousy or under the influence of anger or with an outlook full of differences (or with incorrect outlook of things), is called a Tāmasa type of devotee.

devanagari: विषयान् अभिसन्धाय यश ऐश्वर्यमेव वा । अर्चादौ अर्चयेद्यो मां पृथग्भावः स राजसः ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: viṣayān abhisandhāya yaśa aiśvaryameva vāarcādau arcayedyo māṃ pṛthagbhāvaḥ sa rājasaḥ
translation: He who, with a desire of worldly pleasures or of fame or authoritative power, worships me in my images and entertains notions of difference, is a devotee of rājasa type.

devanagari: कर्मनिर्हारमुद्दिश्य परस्मिन् वा तदर्पणम् । यजेद् यष्टव्यमिति वा पृथग्भावः स सात्त्विकः ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: karmanirhāramuddiśya parasmin vā tadarpaṇamyajed yaṣṭavyamiti vā pṛthagbhāvaḥ sa sāttvikaḥ
translation: [1] He who wishes to purge all (his) karmas, or desires to dedicate them to the Supreme Lord or worships the Lord with the simple objective of worship (and expecting no return for it) but entertains the idea of difference[2], is called a devotee of the Sāttvika type.
purport:
Footnote [1]:
Bhāvāratha Dīpikā and later Siddhāntapradīpa and Bālaprabodhini state that there are 81 types of saguṇa- bhakti. Bhāvāratha Dīpikā details them as follows: 1-3 Tāmasa bhakti—actuated by injury (hiṃsā), religious hypocrisy (dambha) and jealousy (matsara); 4-6 Rājasa bhakti—motivated by the desire for objects of enjoyment, for reputation and for authority; 7-9 Sāttvikī bhakti—generated by a desire to purge all sins, to dedicate karmas to God and to worship as a duty with no ulterior motive. Each of these is subdivided into nine according to the ninefold path of bhakti consisting of Śravaṇa, Kīrtaṇa etc.

Footnote [2]:
Bhāgavata Candrikā & VS. read a-pṛthag-bhāva: (i) The knowledge that he is like the body of the Lord (Bhāgavata Candrikā); (Who sees identity or oneness in my forms.

devanagari: मद्‍गुणश्रुतिमात्रेण मयि सर्वगुहाशये । मनोगतिः अविच्छिन्ना यथा गङ्गाम्भसोऽम्बुधौ ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: mad‍guṇaśrutimātreṇa mayi sarvaguhāśayemanogatiḥ avicchinnā yathā gaṅgāmbhaso'mbudhau
translation: Just as the waters of the Gaṅgā continuously flow into the sea, similarly by merely listening to (the description of) my qualities the mind incessantly flows to me who reside in the hearts of all (beings). This close and intimate devotion to the Lord (Puruṣottama), without any ulterior motive, is definitely regarded as the characteristic of the nirguṇa type of Bhakti yoga.

devanagari: लक्षणं भक्तियोगस्य निर्गुणस्य ह्युदाहृतम् । अहैतुक्यव्यवहिता या भक्तिः पुरुषोत्तमे ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: lakṣaṇaṃ bhaktiyogasya nirguṇasya hyudāhṛtamahaitukyavyavahitā yā bhaktiḥ puruṣottame
translation: Just as the waters of the Gaṅgā continuously flow into the sea, similarly by merely listening to (the description of) my qualities the mind incessantly flows to me who reside in the hearts of all (beings). This close and intimate devotion to the Lord (Puruṣottama), without any ulterior motive, is definitely regarded as the characteristic of the nirguṇa type of Bhakti yoga.

devanagari: सालोक्यसार्ष्टिसामीप्य सारूप्यैकत्वमप्युत । दीयमानं न गृह्णन्ति विना मत्सेवनं जनाः ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: sālokyasārṣṭisāmīpya sārūpyaikatvamapyutadīyamānaṃ na gṛhṇanti vinā matsevanaṃ janāḥ
translation: Even if the kinds of liberation (muktis), viz. residence in the same region with me (sālokya), equality in wealth, power and glory like me (sārṣṭi), staying near me (sāmīpya), similarity of form like me (sārupya [sārūpya?]) and even union with God (ekatva) are offered to these persons (the nirguṇa- bhaktas) they do not accept anything except my service.

devanagari: स एव भक्तियोगाख्य आत्यन्तिक उदाहृतः । येनातिव्रज्य त्रिगुणं मद्‍भावायोपपद्यते ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: sa eva bhaktiyogākhya ātyantika udāhṛtaḥyenātivrajya triguṇaṃ mad‍bhāvāyopapadyate
translation: That only is called the absolute[3] (and the highest) Bhaktiyoga whereby one transcends the trinity of guṇas (and the saṃsāra caused by them) and attains to my state (Brahma- hood).
purport:
Footnote [3]:
ātyantika—That which takes place finally, i.e. sāyujyatā (absorption into God).

devanagari: निषेवितेनानिमित्तेन स्वधर्मेण महीयसा । क्रियायोगेन शस्तेन नातिहिंस्रेण नित्यशः ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: niṣevitenānimittena svadharmeṇa mahīyasākriyāyogena śastena nātihiṃsreṇa nityaśaḥ
translation: The mind of the man (my nirguṇa devotee) becomes purified by careful performance of daily religious duties without any motive, by performance of disinterested worship with ardent faith (as prescribed in the Pañcarātra āgama) without involving the least injury to beings[4].
purport:
Footnote [4]:
nātihiṃsreṇa [nātihiṃsra]—Some hiṃsā (injury) to subtle beings is inevitable while washing the temple, cooking food for offering to God, collecting flowers and fruit etc. for worship. But this injury is condonable.—VC.

devanagari: मद्धिष्ण्य दर्शनस्पर्श पूजास्तुति अभिवन्दनैः । भूतेषु मद्‍भावनया सत्त्वेनासङ्गमेन च ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: maddhiṣṇya darśanasparśa pūjāstuti abhivandanaiḥbhūteṣu mad‍bhāvanayā sattvenāsaṅgamena ca
translation: by beholding at my image, by touching, worshiping praising and bowing to me, by regarding me (as residing) in all beings, by firmness or fortitude and non-attachment.

devanagari: महतां बहुमानेन दीनानां अनुकम्पया । मैत्र्या चैवात्मतुल्येषु यमेन नियमेन च ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: mahatāṃ bahumānena dīnānāṃ anukampayāmaitryā caivātmatulyeṣu yamena niyamena ca
translation: by paying great respect to the great, by showing compassion to the afflicted, by friendliness towards one’s- equals and by observance of yama and niyama.

devanagari: आध्यात्मिकानुश्रवणात् नामसङ्कीर्तनाच्च मे । आर्जवेनार्यसङ्गेन निरहङ्क्रियया तथा ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: ādhyātmikānuśravaṇāt nāmasaṅkīrtanācca meārjavenāryasaṅgena nirahaṅkriyayā tathā
translation: by listening to the philosophic discourses about ātman, by eulogizing the name (of God), by straightforwardness and by association with the noble (Souls) and by giving up ahaṃkāra.

devanagari: मद् धर्मणो गुणैः एतैः परिसंशुद्ध आशयः । पुरुषस्याञ्जसाभ्येति श्रुतमात्रगुणं हि माम् ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: mad dharmaṇo guṇaiḥ etaiḥ parisaṃśuddha āśayaḥpuruṣasyāñjasābhyeti śrutamātraguṇaṃ hi mām
translation: As soon as such a devotee listens to (the description of) my attributes, he is easily attracted to me.

devanagari: यथा वातरथो घ्राणं आवृङ्क्ते गन्ध आशयात् । एवं योगरतं चेत आत्मानं अविकारि यत् ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: yathā vātaratho ghrāṇaṃ āvṛṅkte gandha āśayātevaṃ yogarataṃ ceta ātmānaṃ avikāri yat
translation: Just as fragrance being carried by (the chariot in the form of) the wind from its source (reaches the nose) and captures the smelling organ, similarly the mind which is constant and unperturbed and absorbed in yoga, attains to (and. realizes) the ātman.

devanagari: अहं सर्वेषु भूतेषु भूतात्मावस्थितः सदा । तं अवज्ञाय मां मर्त्यः कुरुतेऽर्चाविडम्बनम् ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: ahaṃ sarveṣu bhūteṣu bhūtātmāvasthitaḥ sadātaṃ avajñāya māṃ martyaḥ kurute'rcāviḍambanam
translation: I am always abiding in all beings as their Soul (antaryāmin). A man who disregards me (the antaryāmin) performs a sham idol-worship.

devanagari: यो मां सर्वेषु भूतेषु सन्तमात्मानमीश्वरम् । हित्वार्चां भजते मौढ्याद् भस्मन्येव जुहोति सः ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: yo māṃ sarveṣu bhūteṣu santamātmānamīśvaramhitvārcāṃ bhajate mauḍhyād bhasmanyeva juhoti saḥ
translation: He who foolishly neglects me, the controller of the world dwelling as a Soul (ātman) in all beings, and worships merely the images, is (as if), offering oblations in ashes (instead of in the fire).

devanagari: द्विषतः परकाये मां मानिनो भिन्नदर्शिनः । भूतेषु बद्धवैरस्य न मनः शान्तिमृच्छति ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: dviṣataḥ parakāye māṃ mānino bhinnadarśinaḥbhūteṣu baddhavairasya na manaḥ śāntimṛcchati
translation: The mind of a man who possesses ahaṃkāra (about his body identifying it with the Soul) and who hates me in another body regarding me as different (in every person) and contracts enmity with other beings, never attains tranquillity.

devanagari: अहमुच्चावचैर्द्रव्यैः क्रिययोत्पन्नयानघे । नैव तुष्येऽर्चितोऽर्चायां भूतग्रामावमानिनः ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: ahamuccāvacairdravyaiḥ kriyayotpannayānaghenaiva tuṣye'rcito'rcāyāṃ bhūtagrāmāvamāninaḥ
translation: Oh sinless, I am not at all pleased with a person who slights all beings (even though) I am worshipped as an image by him, with (ritualistic) offerings of various articles (like sandle-paste, flowers, fruits etc.).

devanagari: अर्चादौ अर्चयेत्तावद् ईश्वरं मां स्वकर्मकृत् । यावन्न वेद स्वहृदि सर्वभूतेष्ववस्थितम् ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: arcādau arcayettāvad īśvaraṃ māṃ svakarmakṛtyāvanna veda svahṛdi sarvabhūteṣvavasthitam
translation: So long as a man does not realize in his heart that I, the Supreme Lord, am present in all beings he should perform his religious duties and worship me in an idol.

devanagari: आत्मनश्च परस्यापि यः करोत्यन्तरोदरम् । तस्य भिन्नदृशो मृत्युः विदधे भयमुल्बणम् ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: ātmanaśca parasyāpi yaḥ karotyantarodaramtasya bhinnadṛśo mṛtyuḥ vidadhe bhayamulbaṇam
translation: [5] If a person makes even a slight difference between himself and the Supreme Self (Paramātman)[6], I, who am Death, create a big fear for him who entertains the idea of difference.
purport:
Footnote [5]:
Kramasandarbha. & VC. take udara = stomach. Kramasandarbha., VC.: One should treat a hungry man like himself and feed him properly.
Padaratnāvalī: Mṛtyu in the from of Nṛsiṃha creates great fear (viz. throwing a man in the darkest hell) for the man who regarḍs all the antaryāmim (inner Souls of beings) as different.

Footnote [6]:
Or: who regards body as coming in between his individual Soul and the Supreme Soul—Bhāvāratha Dīpikā

devanagari: अथ मां सर्वभूतेषु भूतात्मानं कृतालयम् । अर्हयेद् दानमानाभ्यां मैत्र्याभिन्नेन चक्षुषा ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: atha māṃ sarvabhūteṣu bhūtātmānaṃ kṛtālayamarhayed dānamānābhyāṃ maitryābhinnena cakṣuṣā
translation: Hence he should worship me by gifts and respectful behaviour, friendliness and equality of outlook—me who dwell in all beings as their Soul.

devanagari: जीवाः श्रेष्ठा हि अजीवानां ततः प्राणभृतः शुभे । ततः सचित्ताः प्रवराः ततश्चेन्द्रियवृत्तयः ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: jīvāḥ śreṣṭhā hi ajīvānāṃ tataḥ prāṇabhṛtaḥ śubhetataḥ sacittāḥ pravarāḥ tataścendriyavṛttayaḥ
translation: Oh auspicious mother, sentient beings are superior to non-sentient ones. Those who bear Prāṇa (have vital functions of respiration etc.) are superior to the sentient beings. To them are superior the possessors of citta. Those who possess the function of the sense organs are higher than the possessors of mere citta (mind).

devanagari: तत्रापि स्पर्शवेदिभ्यः प्रवरा रसवेदिनः । तेभ्यो गन्धविदः श्रेष्ठाः ततः शब्दविदो वराः ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: tatrāpi sparśavedibhyaḥ pravarā rasavedinaḥtebhyo gandhavidaḥ śreṣṭhāḥ tataḥ śabdavido varāḥ
translation: Even amongst the possessors of sense-organs those who possess the sense of taste are higher than those who possess the sense of touch. To these are superior those who possess the sense of smell. Higher than these are the possessors of the sense of hearing.

devanagari: रूपभेदविदस्तत्र ततश्चोभयतोदतः । तेषां बहुपदाः श्रेष्ठाः चतुष्पादस्ततो द्विपात् ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: rūpabhedavidastatra tataścobhayatodataḥteṣāṃ bahupadāḥ śreṣṭhāḥ catuṣpādastato dvipāt
translation: Beings which know the difference in colour and forms (i.e. have the eyes) are higher than those possessing the sense of hearing. To these are superior, the beings who have teeth in the lower and upper jaws. Higher than these are the multipeds. Quadruped animals are superior to multipeds and bipeds are higher than the quadrupeds.

devanagari: ततो वर्णाश्च चत्वारः तेषां ब्राह्मण उत्तमः । ब्राह्मणेष्वपि वेदज्ञो हि अर्थज्ञोऽभ्यधिकस्ततः ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: tato varṇāśca catvāraḥ teṣāṃ brāhmaṇa uttamaḥbrāhmaṇeṣvapi vedajño hi arthajño'bhyadhikastataḥ
translation: Among the bipeds, four Varṇas (castes) are superior and the Brāhmaṇas are the highest among them (the castes). Among the Brāhmaṇas, the reciters of the Vedas are higher. Superior to these are those who understand the meaning of the Vedas.

devanagari: अर्थज्ञात्संशयच्छेत्ता ततः श्रेयान् स्वकर्मकृत् । मुक्तसङ्गस्ततो भूयात् अदोग्धा धर्ममात्मनः ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: arthajñātsaṃśayacchettā tataḥ śreyān svakarmakṛtmuktasaṅgastato bhūyāt adogdhā dharmamātmanaḥ
translation: He who can solve doubts and difficulties (of Vedic interpretation) is superior to him who simply knows the meaning (of the Veda). To him is superior the performer of one’s prescribed religious duties. But a person who has renounced all association and does not desire the fruit of his religious acts is higher than the previous one.

devanagari: तस्मान्मय्यर्पिताशेष क्रियार्थात्मा निरन्तरः । मय्यर्पितात्मनः पुंसो मयि सन्न्यस्तकर्मणः । न पश्यामि परं भूतं अकर्तुः समदर्शनात् ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: tasmānmayyarpitāśeṣa kriyārthātmā nirantaraḥmayyarpitātmanaḥ puṃso mayi sannyastakarmaṇaḥna paśyāmi paraṃ bhūtaṃ akartuḥ samadarśanāt
translation: Superior to him is the man who has dedicated all his actions and body to me and who does not entertain the notion of difference. I do not know anyone superior to a person who has dedicated his body to me, deposited his karmas in me, and has no ego of being the agent of any action and who sees all as equals.

devanagari: मनसैतानि भूतानि प्रणमेद्‍बहुमानयन् । ईश्वरो जीवकलया प्रविष्टो भगवानिति ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: manasaitāni bhūtāni praṇamed‍bahumānayanīśvaro jīvakalayā praviṣṭo bhagavāniti
translation: One should understand that the glorious Lord himself has entered all beings as their jīva (individual Soul) and should pay great respects to them mentally and bow down to them.

devanagari: भक्तियोगश्च योगश्च मया मानव्युदीरितः । ययोरेकतरेणैव पुरुषः पुरुषं व्रजेत् ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: bhaktiyogaśca yogaśca mayā mānavyudīritaḥyayorekatareṇaiva puruṣaḥ puruṣaṃ vrajet
translation: Oh daughter of Manu, the Bhakti-yoga (the path of Bhakti) and Yoga (of eight stages) have been explained to you by me. By following one of them, a man will attain to God (the Supreme Man).

devanagari: एतद्‍भगवतो रूपं ब्रह्मणः परमात्मनः । परं प्रधानं पुरुषं दैवं कर्मविचेष्टितम् ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: etad‍bhagavato rūpaṃ brahmaṇaḥ paramātmanaḥparaṃ pradhānaṃ puruṣaṃ daivaṃ karmaviceṣṭitam
translation: This is the form of the glorious Lord, the Supreme Soul, the Brahman. It is both Prakṛti and Puruṣa (and still) is also beyond them. It is the unseen destiny (daiva) which is the cause of all karmas (in the form of saṃsāra).

devanagari: रूपभेदास्पदं दिव्यं काल इत्यभिधीयते । भूतानां महदादीनां यतो भिन्नदृशां भयम् ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: rūpabhedāspadaṃ divyaṃ kāla ityabhidhīyatebhūtānāṃ mahadādīnāṃ yato bhinnadṛśāṃ bhayam
translation: The divine form (of the Lord) which is the cause of the differences in the appearances of things, is called Time. From it, fear is caused to beings, which entertain the notion of difference and which preside over the Mahat and others.

devanagari: योऽन्तः प्रविश्य भूतानि भूतैरत्त्यखिलाश्रयः । स विष्ण्वाख्योऽधियज्ञोऽसौ कालः कलयतां प्रभुः ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: yo'ntaḥ praviśya bhūtāni bhūtairattyakhilāśrayaḥsa viṣṇvākhyo'dhiyajño'sau kālaḥ kalayatāṃ prabhuḥ
translation: He enters into all beings (bhūtas) and supports them all. He eats them up (annihilates them) by their means. He is called Viṣṇu, presiding deity of sacrifices who confers the fruit of the sacrifice (on the performer). He is the Time, the ruler of rulers.

devanagari: न चास्य कश्चिद् दयितो न द्वेष्यो न च बान्धवः । आविशत्यप्रमत्तोऽसौ प्रमत्तं जनमन्तकृत् ॥ ३९ ॥
english_devnagari: na cāsya kaścid dayito na dveṣyo na ca bāndhavaḥāviśatyapramatto'sau pramattaṃ janamantakṛt
translation: Nobody is dear or inimical to him. He has no friend or relative. He is always alert and enters into the negligent people in order to destroy them.

devanagari: यद्‍भयाद् वाति वातोऽयं सूर्यस्तपति यद्‍भयात् । यद्‍भयाद् वर्षते देवो भगणो भाति यद्‍भयात् ॥ ४० ॥
english_devnagari: yad‍bhayād vāti vāto'yaṃ sūryastapati yad‍bhayātyad‍bhayād varṣate devo bhagaṇo bhāti yad‍bhayāt
translation: It is out of his (Kāla’s) fear that the wind blows. It is due to his fear that the Sun shines. God Indra showers (water) out of his fear. Heavenly bodies shine out of his dread.

devanagari: यद् वनस्पतयो भीता लताश्चौषधिभिः सह । स्वे स्वे कालेऽभिगृह्णन्ति पुष्पाणि च फलानि च ॥ ४१ ॥
english_devnagari: yad vanaspatayo bhītā latāścauṣadhibhiḥ sahasve sve kāle'bhigṛhṇanti puṣpāṇi ca phalāni ca
translation: It is due to his fear that trees, creepers, plants and herbs blossom forth and bear fruits in the proper seasons.

devanagari: स्रवन्ति सरितो भीता नोत्सर्पत्युदधिर्यतः । अग्निरिन्धे सगिरिभिः भूर्न मज्जति यद्‍भयात् ॥ ४२ ॥
english_devnagari: sravanti sarito bhītā notsarpatyudadhiryataḥagnirindhe sagiribhiḥ bhūrna majjati yad‍bhayāt
translation: It is out of his fear that the rivers flow and the sea does not overflow his fixed limits. Being afraid of him, the fire burns and the earth (burdened) with mountains does not submerge (in the sea).

devanagari: नभो ददाति श्वसतां पदं यन्नियमाददः । लोकं स्वदेहं तनुते महान् सप्तभिरावृतम् ॥ ४३ ॥
english_devnagari: nabho dadāti śvasatāṃ padaṃ yanniyamādadaḥlokaṃ svadehaṃ tanute mahān saptabhirāvṛtam
translation: It is due to his control that the sky affords space for living (breathing) creatures and the Principle Mahat expands its body into the world enveloped in seven sheaths.

devanagari: गुणाभिमानिनो देवाः सर्गादिष्वस्य यद्‍भयात् । वर्तन्तेऽनुयुगं येषां वश एतच्चराचरम् ॥ ४४ ॥
english_devnagari: guṇābhimānino devāḥ sargādiṣvasya yad‍bhayātvartante'nuyugaṃ yeṣāṃ vaśa etaccarācaram
translation: It is out of his fear that (Brahmā and other) gods who preside over the guṇas (like sattva etc.) and who can control this mobile and immobile world (creatures), carry out their duties of creation etc. in every Yuga.

devanagari: सोऽनन्तोऽन्तकरः कालो अनादिरादिकृदव्ययः । जनं जनेन जनयन् मारयन् मृत्युनान्तकम् ॥ ४५ ॥
english_devnagari: so'nanto'ntakaraḥ kālo anādirādikṛdavyayaḥjanaṃ janena janayan mārayan mṛtyunāntakam
translation:
He is endless but puts an end (to all). Time is beginningless but marks the beginning of all. He is immutable. He causes beings to be born of parents and causes the end of antaka (god of death) by means of death.
[back to top]
